motivation: 传统Transformer在3D原子图任务（如分子预测）中表现不佳，即使引入平移不变性等先验知识，因此需设计适应3D结构的等变图神经网络。
method: 将Transformer操作替换为等变版本并引入张量积，设计基于不可约表示的等变信息编码；提出多层感知机注意力机制替代点积注意力，结合非线性消息传递。
result: 在QM9、MD17和OC20数据集上，Equiformer达到与现有模型相当的性能，验证了其有效性。
conclusion: Equiformer通过等变操作与改进注意力机制，成功将Transformer扩展至3D原子图领域，为分子建模提供新方法。
search_text: Equiformer: Equivariant Graph Attention Transformer for 3D Atomistic Graphs Equiformer：3D 原子图的等变图注意力变换器 Despite their widespread success in various domains, Transformer networks have yet to perform well across datasets in the domain of 3D atomistic graphs such as molecules even when 3D-related inductive biases like translational invariance and rotational equivariance are considered. In this paper, we demonstrate that Transformers can generalize well to 3D atomistic graphs and present Equiformer, a graph neural network leveraging the strength of Transformer architectures and incorporating SE(3)/E(3)-equivariant features based on irreducible representations (irreps). First, we propose a simple and effective architecture by only replacing original operations in Transformers with their equivariant counterparts and including tensor products. Using equivariant operations enables encoding equivariant information in channels of irreps features without complicating graph structures. With minimal modifications to Transformers, this architecture has already achieved strong empirical results. Second, we propose a novel attention mechanism called equivariant graph attention, which improves upon typical attention in Transformers through replacing dot product attention with multi-layer perceptron attention and including non-linear message passing. With these two innovations, Equiformer achieves competitive results to previous models on QM9, MD17 and OC20 datasets. 尽管 Transformer 网络在各个领域取得了广泛的成功，但即使考虑到平移不变性和旋转等变性等与 3D 相关的归纳偏差，Transformer 网络在分子等 3D 原子图领域的数据集上也尚未表现良好。 In this paper, we demonstrate that Transformers can generalize well to 3D atomistic graphs and present Equiformer, a graph neural network leveraging the strength of Transformer architectures and incorporating SE(3)/E(3)-equivariant features based on irreducible representations (irreps).首先，我们提出了一种简单而有效的架构，仅用其等变对应物替换 Transformer 中的原始操作并包含张量积。 Using equivariant operations enables encoding equivariant information in channels of irreps features without complicating graph structures. With minimal modifications to Transformers, this architecture has already achieved strong empirical results.其次，我们提出了一种称为等变图注意力的新颖注意力机制，它通过用多层感知器注意力取代点积注意力并包括非线性消息传递来改进 Transformer 中的典型注意力。 With these two innovations, Equiformer achieves competitive results to previous models on QM9, MD17 and OC20 datasets. 本文提出Equiformer模型，通过结合Transformer架构与SE(3)/E(3)等变特征，解决3D原子图（如分子）建模中的性能不足问题。 Yi-Lun Liao,Tess Smidt cs.LG,cs.AI,physics.comp-ph

authors_text: Aditya Kulkarni,Min Kim,Joydeep Bhattacharya,Jayanta Bhattacharya
title: Businesses in high-income zip codes often saw sharper visit reductions during the COVID-19 pandemic
title_zh: 在 COVID-19 大流行期间，高收入邮政编码区的企业访问量通常会大幅减少
abstract: As the COVID-19 pandemic unfolded, the mobility patterns of people worldwide changed drastically. While travel time, costs, and trip convenience have always influenced mobility, the risk of infection and policy actions such as lockdowns and stay-at-home orders emerged as new factors to consider in the location-visitation calculus. We use SafeGraph mobility data from Minnesota, USA, to demonstrate that businesses (especially those requiring extended indoor visits) located in affluent zip codes witnessed sharper reductions in visits (relative to pre-pandemic times) outside of the lockdown periods than their poorer counterparts. To the extent visits translate into sales, we contend that post-pandemic recovery efforts should prioritize relief funding, keeping the losses relating to diminished visits in mind.
summary: As the COVID-19 pandemic unfolded, the mobility patterns of people worldwide changed drastically. While travel time, costs, and trip convenience have always influenced mobility, the risk of infection and policy actions such as lockdowns and stay-at-home orders emerged as new factors to consider in the location-visitation calculus. We use SafeGraph mobility data from Minnesota, USA, to demonstrate that businesses (especially those requiring extended indoor visits) located in affluent zip codes witnessed sharper reductions in visits (relative to pre-pandemic times) outside of the lockdown periods than their poorer counterparts. To the extent visits translate into sales, we contend that post-pandemic recovery efforts should prioritize relief funding, keeping the losses relating to diminished visits in mind.
abstract_zh: 随着 COVID-19 大流行的蔓延，世界各地人们的流动模式发生了巨大变化。虽然旅行时间、成本和旅行便利性始终影响出行，但感染风险和封锁和居家令等政策行动成为位置访问计算中需要考虑的新因素。我们使用美国明尼苏达州的 SafeGraph 移动数据来证明，位于富裕邮政编码区的企业（尤其是那些需要长时间室内访问的企业）在封锁期之外的访问量（相对于大流行前的时间）比较贫穷的企业出现了更大幅度的减少。就访问转化为销售而言，我们认为大流行后的恢复工作应优先考虑救济资金，同时牢记与访问减少相关的损失。

AI.tldr: 论文分析疫情期间高收入地区企业访客量下降现象，探讨其影响及恢复措施。
AI.motivation: 疫情期间出行模式变化，传统因素与新因素（感染风险、政策）影响企业访客量，需了解不同收入地区企业的差异以指导恢复资金分配。
AI.method: 使用明尼苏达州SafeGraph移动数据，比较高收入与低收入邮编地区企业访客量变化，特别关注封锁期外的数据差异。
AI.result: 高收入地区企业访客量下降更显著，尤其需要长时间室内停留的业务，访客量减少可能影响销售额。
AI.conclusion: 灾后恢复应优先考虑高收入地区企业援助，以弥补访客减少带来的损失，促进经济复苏。

authors_text: Lionel Nganyewou Tidjon,Foutse Khomh
title: Never trust, always verify : a roadmap for Trustworthy AI?
title_zh: 永远不要信任，永远验证：值得信赖的人工智能的路线图？
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is becoming the corner stone of many systems used in our daily lives such as autonomous vehicles, healthcare systems, and unmanned aircraft systems. Machine Learning is a field of AI that enables systems to learn from data and make decisions on new data based on models to achieve a given goal. The stochastic nature of AI models makes verification and validation tasks challenging. Moreover, there are intrinsic biaises in AI models such as reproductibility bias, selection bias (e.g., races, genders, color), and reporting bias (i.e., results that do not reflect the reality). Increasingly, there is also a particular attention to the ethical, legal, and societal impacts of AI. AI systems are difficult to audit and certify because of their black-box nature. They also appear to be vulnerable to threats; AI systems can misbehave when untrusted data are given, making them insecure and unsafe. Governments, national and international organizations have proposed several principles to overcome these challenges but their applications in practice are limited and there are different interpretations in the principles that can bias implementations. In this paper, we examine trust in the context of AI-based systems to understand what it means for an AI system to be trustworthy and identify actions that need to be undertaken to ensure that AI systems are trustworthy. To achieve this goal, we first review existing approaches proposed for ensuring the trustworthiness of AI systems, in order to identify potential conceptual gaps in understanding what trustworthy AI is. Then, we suggest a trust (resp. zero-trust) model for AI and suggest a set of properties that should be satisfied to ensure the trustworthiness of AI systems.
summary: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is becoming the corner stone of many systems used in our daily lives such as autonomous vehicles, healthcare systems, and unmanned aircraft systems. Machine Learning is a field of AI that enables systems to learn from data and make decisions on new data based on models to achieve a given goal. The stochastic nature of AI models makes verification and validation tasks challenging. Moreover, there are intrinsic biaises in AI models such as reproductibility bias, selection bias (e.g., races, genders, color), and reporting bias (i.e., results that do not reflect the reality). Increasingly, there is also a particular attention to the ethical, legal, and societal impacts of AI. AI systems are difficult to audit and certify because of their black-box nature. They also appear to be vulnerable to threats; AI systems can misbehave when untrusted data are given, making them insecure and unsafe. Governments, national and international organizations have proposed several principles to overcome these challenges but their applications in practice are limited and there are different interpretations in the principles that can bias implementations. In this paper, we examine trust in the context of AI-based systems to understand what it means for an AI system to be trustworthy and identify actions that need to be undertaken to ensure that AI systems are trustworthy. To achieve this goal, we first review existing approaches proposed for ensuring the trustworthiness of AI systems, in order to identify potential conceptual gaps in understanding what trustworthy AI is. Then, we suggest a trust (resp. zero-trust) model for AI and suggest a set of properties that should be satisfied to ensure the trustworthiness of AI systems.
abstract_zh: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is becoming the corner stone of many systems used in our daily lives such as autonomous vehicles, healthcare systems, and unmanned aircraft systems. Machine Learning is a field of AI that enables systems to learn from data and make decisions on new data based on models to achieve a given goal.人工智能模型的随机性使得验证和确认任务具有挑战性。 Moreover, there are intrinsic biaises in AI models such as reproductibility bias, selection bias (e.g., races, genders, color), and reporting bias (i.e., results that do not reflect the reality).人们也越来越关注人工智能的道德、法律和社会影响。人工智能系统由于其黑匣子性质而难以审计和认证。他们似乎也容易受到威胁；当提供不受信任的数据时，人工智能系统可能会行为不当，从而使其不安全。 Governments, national and international organizations have proposed several principles to overcome these challenges but their applications in practice are limited and there are different interpretations in the principles that can bias implementations. In this paper, we examine trust in the context of AI-based systems to understand what it means for an AI system to be trustworthy and identify actions that need to be undertaken to ensure that AI systems are trustworthy.为了实现这一目标，我们首先回顾为确保人工智能系统的可信度而提出的现有方法，以确定在理解什么是值得信赖的人工智能时潜在的概念差距。然后，我们提出了人工智能的信任（或零信任）模型，并提出了一组应满足的属性，以确保人工智能系统的可信度。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了人工智能系统可信度的内涵，提出了一种信任（或零信任）模型及确保AI系统可信的属性集合。
AI.motivation: AI系统因黑箱特性、数据偏差及伦理法律风险而难以审计和认证，现有原则缺乏实践指导性，亟需明确可信AI的定义与实现路径。
AI.method: 通过回顾现有可信AI方法，识别概念性缺口，进而提出基于信任/零信任模型的框架及需满足的系统属性。
AI.result: 明确了可信AI的核心属性，提出了可操作的信任模型，为后续研究与实践提供了路线图。
AI.conclusion: 本文为构建可信AI系统提供了理论框架与实践方向，强调需通过严格验证机制应对AI的不确定性和潜在风险。

authors_text: Diane Uwacu,Ananya Yammanuru,Marco Morales,Nancy M. Amato
title: Hierarchical Planning with Annotated Skeleton Guidance
title_zh: 带注释骨架指导的分层规划
abstract: We present a hierarchical skeleton-guided motion planning algorithm to guide mobile robots. A good skeleton maps the connectivity of the subspace of c-space containing significant degrees of freedom and is able to guide the planner to find the desired solutions fast. However, sometimes the skeleton does not closely represent the free c-space, which often misleads current skeleton-guided planners. The hierarchical skeleton-guided planning strategy gradually relaxes its reliance on the workspace skeleton as C space is sampled, thereby incrementally returning a sub-optimal path, a feature that is not guaranteed in the standard skeleton-guided algorithm. Experimental comparisons to the standard skeleton-guided planners and other lazy planning strategies show significant improvement in roadmap construction run time while maintaining path quality for multi-query problems in cluttered environments.
summary: We present a hierarchical skeleton-guided motion planning algorithm to guide mobile robots. A good skeleton maps the connectivity of the subspace of c-space containing significant degrees of freedom and is able to guide the planner to find the desired solutions fast. However, sometimes the skeleton does not closely represent the free c-space, which often misleads current skeleton-guided planners. The hierarchical skeleton-guided planning strategy gradually relaxes its reliance on the workspace skeleton as C space is sampled, thereby incrementally returning a sub-optimal path, a feature that is not guaranteed in the standard skeleton-guided algorithm. Experimental comparisons to the standard skeleton-guided planners and other lazy planning strategies show significant improvement in roadmap construction run time while maintaining path quality for multi-query problems in cluttered environments.
abstract_zh: We present a hierarchical skeleton-guided motion planning algorithm to guide mobile robots.一个好的骨架映射了包含显着自由度的 c 空间子空间的连通性，并且能够指导规划器快速找到所需的解决方案。 However, sometimes the skeleton does not closely represent the free c-space, which often misleads current skeleton-guided planners.随着 C 空间的采样，分层骨架引导规划策略逐渐放松对工作空间骨架的依赖，从而增量返回次优路径，这是标准骨架引导算法中无法保证的功能。与标准骨架引导规划器和其他惰性规划策略的实验比较表明，路线图构建运行时间显着改善，同时保持杂乱环境中多查询问题的路径质量。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种分层骨架引导运动规划算法，用于提升移动机器人在复杂环境中的路径规划效率。
AI.motivation: 现有骨架引导规划器因骨架与自由C空间不匹配易导致路径误导，且无法保证次优路径生成。
AI.method: 采用分层策略逐步降低对骨架的依赖，通过采样C空间增量生成次优路径，结合注释骨架进行引导。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在杂乱环境多查询问题中显著提升路网构建速度，同时保持路径质量。
AI.conclusion: 分层骨架引导规划策略有效解决了传统方法的局限性，在效率与路径质量间取得平衡。

search_text: Hierarchical Planning with Annotated Skeleton Guidance 带注释骨架指导的分层规划 We present a hierarchical skeleton-guided motion planning algorithm to guide mobile robots. A good skeleton maps the connectivity of the subspace of c-space containing significant degrees of freedom and is able to guide the planner to find the desired solutions fast. However, sometimes the skeleton does not closely represent the free c-space, which often misleads current skeleton-guided planners. The hierarchical skeleton-guided planning strategy gradually relaxes its reliance on the workspace skeleton as C space is sampled, thereby incrementally returning a sub-optimal path, a feature that is not guaranteed in the standard skeleton-guided algorithm. Experimental comparisons to the standard skeleton-guided planners and other lazy planning strategies show significant improvement in roadmap construction run time while maintaining path quality for multi-query problems in cluttered environments. authors_text: Tilak Purohit,Imen Ben Mahmoud,Bogdan Vlasenko,Mathew Magimai. -Doss
title: Comparing supervised and self-supervised embedding for ExVo Multi-Task learning track
title_zh: 比较 ExVo 多任务学习轨道的监督嵌入和自监督嵌入
comment: ef:Proceedings of the ICML 2022 Expressive Vocalizations Workshop and Competition: Recognizing, Generating, and Personalizing Vocal Bursts
comments: ef:Proceedings of the ICML 2022 Expressive Vocalizations Workshop and Competition: Recognizing, Generating, and Personalizing Vocal Bursts
abstract: The ICML Expressive Vocalizations (ExVo) Multi-task challenge 2022, focuses on understanding the emotional facets of the non-linguistic vocalizations (vocal bursts (VB)). The objective of this challenge is to predict emotional intensities for VB, being a multi-task challenge it also requires to predict speakers' age and native-country. For this challenge we study and compare two distinct embedding spaces namely, self-supervised learning (SSL) based embeddings and task-specific supervised learning based embeddings. Towards that, we investigate feature representations obtained from several pre-trained SSL neural networks and task-specific supervised classification neural networks. Our studies show that the best performance is obtained with a hybrid approach, where predictions derived via both SSL and task-specific supervised learning are used. Our best system on test-set surpasses the ComPARE baseline (harmonic mean of all sub-task scores i.e., $S_{MTL}$) by a relative $13\%$ margin.
summary: The ICML Expressive Vocalizations (ExVo) Multi-task challenge 2022, focuses on understanding the emotional facets of the non-linguistic vocalizations (vocal bursts (VB)). The objective of this challenge is to predict emotional intensities for VB, being a multi-task challenge it also requires to predict speakers' age and native-country. For this challenge we study and compare two distinct embedding spaces namely, self-supervised learning (SSL) based embeddings and task-specific supervised learning based embeddings. Towards that, we investigate feature representations obtained from several pre-trained SSL neural networks and task-specific supervised classification neural networks. Our studies show that the best performance is obtained with a hybrid approach, where predictions derived via both SSL and task-specific supervised learning are used. Our best system on test-set surpasses the ComPARE baseline (harmonic mean of all sub-task scores i.e., $S_{MTL}$) by a relative $13\%$ margin.
abstract_zh: 2022 年 ICML 表达性发声 (ExVo) 多任务挑战赛的重点是理解非语言发声（爆发声 (VB)）的情感方面。该挑战的目标是预测 VB 的情绪强度，这是一项多任务挑战，还需要预测说话者的年龄和国籍。对于这一挑战，我们研究并比较了两种不同的嵌入空间，即基于自监督学习（SSL）的嵌入和基于特定任务的监督学习的嵌入。为此，我们研究了从几个预先训练的 SSL 神经网络和特定于任务的监督分类神经网络获得的特征表示。我们的研究表明，最好的性能是通过混合方法获得的，其中使用了通过 SSL 和特定于任务的监督学习得出的预测。我们在测试集上的最佳系统超过了 ComPARE 基线（所有子任务分数的调和平均值，即 $S_{MTL}$），相对差距为 $13\%$。

AI.tldr: 该论文比较了自监督学习（SSL）和任务特定监督学习嵌入在ExVo多任务学习挑战中的表现，旨在提升非语言语音爆发情感强度、年龄和母语的预测效果。
AI.motivation: ExVo挑战要求同时预测语音爆发的情感强度、说话者年龄和母语，需探索更优的嵌入方法以提升多任务学习性能。
AI.method: 通过对比多种预训练SSL模型和任务特定监督分类模型的特征表示，提出结合SSL与监督学习预测结果的混合方法。
AI.result: 混合方法在测试集上超越ComPARE基线（多任务子任务得分调和均值）13%。
AI.conclusion: 混合SSL与监督学习方法能有效提升多任务性能，表明两种嵌入方式各有优势且互补。

authors_text: Xueyi Liu,Yu Rong,Tingyang Xu,Fuchun Sun,Wenbing Huang,Junzhou Huang
title: Similarity-aware Positive Instance Sampling for Graph Contrastive Pre-training
title_zh: 用于图对比预训练的相似性感知正实例采样
abstract: Graph instance contrastive learning has been proved as an effective task for Graph Neural Network (GNN) pre-training. However, one key issue may seriously impede the representative power in existing works: Positive instances created by current methods often miss crucial information of graphs or even yield illegal instances (such as non-chemically-aware graphs in molecular generation). To remedy this issue, we propose to select positive graph instances directly from existing graphs in the training set, which ultimately maintains the legality and similarity to the target graphs. Our selection is based on certain domain-specific pair-wise similarity measurements as well as sampling from a hierarchical graph encoding similarity relations among graphs. Besides, we develop an adaptive node-level pre-training method to dynamically mask nodes to distribute them evenly in the graph. We conduct extensive experiments on $13$ graph classification and node classification benchmark datasets from various domains. The results demonstrate that the GNN models pre-trained by our strategies can outperform those trained-from-scratch models as well as the variants obtained by existing methods.
summary: Graph instance contrastive learning has been proved as an effective task for Graph Neural Network (GNN) pre-training. However, one key issue may seriously impede the representative power in existing works: Positive instances created by current methods often miss crucial information of graphs or even yield illegal instances (such as non-chemically-aware graphs in molecular generation). To remedy this issue, we propose to select positive graph instances directly from existing graphs in the training set, which ultimately maintains the legality and similarity to the target graphs. Our selection is based on certain domain-specific pair-wise similarity measurements as well as sampling from a hierarchical graph encoding similarity relations among graphs. Besides, we develop an adaptive node-level pre-training method to dynamically mask nodes to distribute them evenly in the graph. We conduct extensive experiments on $13$ graph classification and node classification benchmark datasets from various domains. The results demonstrate that the GNN models pre-trained by our strategies can outperform those trained-from-scratch models as well as the variants obtained by existing methods.
abstract_zh: Graph instance contrastive learning has been proved as an effective task for Graph Neural Network (GNN) pre-training.然而，一个关键问题可能会严重阻碍现有工作的代表性：当前方法创建的正例常常会错过图的关键信息，甚至产生非法实例（例如分子生成中的非化学感知图）。为了解决这个问题，我们建议直接从训练集中的现有图中选择正图实例，这最终保持了与目标图的合法性和相似性。我们的选择基于某些特定领域的成对相似性测量以及从编码图之间相似性关系的分层图中采样。 Besides, we develop an adaptive node-level pre-training method to dynamically mask nodes to distribute them evenly in the graph. We conduct extensive experiments on $13$ graph classification and node classification benchmark datasets from various domains.结果表明，通过我们的策略预训练的 GNN 模型可以优于那些从头开始训练的模型以及通过现有方法获得的变体。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于相似性的正样本选择方法，用于改进图对比预训练中正样本的生成问题。
AI.motivation: 现有图对比学习方法生成的正样本可能丢失关键信息或产生非法实例（如化学不合理的分子图），影响预训练效果。
AI.method: 直接从训练集现有图中选取正样本，结合领域特定相似度测量与分层图编码相似性关系进行采样，并设计自适应节点掩码方法动态平衡节点分布。
AI.result: 在13个图分类基准数据集上，预训练模型性能优于从头训练模型及现有方法变体。
AI.conclusion: 基于相似性感知的正样本选择策略能有效提升图神经网络预训练效果，确保样本合法性与相似性。

We conduct extensive experiments on $13$ graph classification and node classification benchmark datasets from various domains. Graph instance contrastive learning has been proved as an effective task for Graph Neural Network (GNN) pre-training.然而，一个关键问题可能会严重阻碍现有工作的代表性：当前方法创建的正例常常会错过图的关键信息，甚至产生非法实例（例如分子生成中的非化学感知图）。为了解决这个问题，我们建议直接从训练集中的现有图中选择正图实例，这最终保持了与目标图的合法性和相似性。我们的选择基于某些特定领域的成对相似性测量以及从编码图之间相似性关系的分层图中采样。 Besides, we develop an adaptive node-level pre-training method to dynamically mask nodes to distribute them evenly in the graph. We conduct extensive experiments on $13$ graph classification and node classification benchmark datasets from various domains.结果表明，通过我们的策略预训练的 GNN 模型可以优于那些从头开始训练的模型以及通过现有方法获得的变体。 本文提出一种基于相似性的正样本选择方法，用于改进图对比预训练中正样本的生成问题。 Xueyi Liu,Yu Rong,Tingyang Xu,Fuchun Sun,Wenbing Huang,Junzhou Huang cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Dylan Ebert,Chen Sun,Ellie Pavlick
title: Do Trajectories Encode Verb Meaning?
title_zh: 轨迹编码动词含义吗？
abstract: Distributional models learn representations of words from text, but are criticized for their lack of grounding, or the linking of text to the non-linguistic world. Grounded language models have had success in learning to connect concrete categories like nouns and adjectives to the world via images and videos, but can struggle to isolate the meaning of the verbs themselves from the context in which they typically occur. In this paper, we investigate the extent to which trajectories (i.e. the position and rotation of objects over time) naturally encode verb semantics. We build a procedurally generated agent-object-interaction dataset, obtain human annotations for the verbs that occur in this data, and compare several methods for representation learning given the trajectories. We find that trajectories correlate as-is with some verbs (e.g., fall), and that additional abstraction via self-supervised pretraining can further capture nuanced differences in verb meaning (e.g., roll vs. slide).
summary: Distributional models learn representations of words from text, but are criticized for their lack of grounding, or the linking of text to the non-linguistic world. Grounded language models have had success in learning to connect concrete categories like nouns and adjectives to the world via images and videos, but can struggle to isolate the meaning of the verbs themselves from the context in which they typically occur. In this paper, we investigate the extent to which trajectories (i.e. the position and rotation of objects over time) naturally encode verb semantics. We build a procedurally generated agent-object-interaction dataset, obtain human annotations for the verbs that occur in this data, and compare several methods for representation learning given the trajectories. We find that trajectories correlate as-is with some verbs (e.g., fall), and that additional abstraction via self-supervised pretraining can further capture nuanced differences in verb meaning (e.g., roll vs. slide).
abstract_zh: 分布式模型从文本中学习单词的表示，但因其缺乏基础或文本与非语言世界的联系而受到批评。 Grounded language models have had success in learning to connect concrete categories like nouns and adjectives to the world via images and videos, but can struggle to isolate the meaning of the verbs themselves from the context in which they typically occur. In this paper, we investigate the extent to which trajectories (i.e. the position and rotation of objects over time) naturally encode verb semantics.我们构建了一个按程序生成的代理-对象交互数据集，获取该数据中出现的动词的人工注释，并比较了给定轨迹的表示学习的几种方法。我们发现轨迹与一些动词（例如，下降）相关，并且通过自监督预训练进行的额外抽象可以进一步捕获动词含义的细微差别（例如，滚动与滑动）。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨轨迹（物体随时间的位置和旋转）是否能自然编码动词语义，并通过构建交互数据集和对比学习方法进行验证。
AI.motivation: 分布模型因缺乏对非语言世界的关联性而受批评，而动词的语义常依赖上下文难以分离，需探索轨迹是否能提供动词的 grounding。
AI.method: 构建程序生成的智能体-物体交互数据集，收集人类动词标注，并比较基于轨迹的表征学习方法，包括自监督预训练。
AI.result: 轨迹直接关联部分动词（如'fall'），而自监督预训练能捕捉更细微的动词差异（如'roll'与'slide'）。
AI.conclusion: 轨迹可编码动词语义，尤其通过抽象化预训练能增强对动词细微差别的捕捉，为动词 grounding 提供新方向。

authors_text: Abiramy Kuganesan,Shih-yang Su,James J. Little,Helge Rhodin
title: UNeRF: Time and Memory Conscious U-Shaped Network for Training Neural Radiance Fields
title_zh: UNeRF：用于训练神经辐射场的时间和记忆意识 U 形网络
abstract: Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs) increase reconstruction detail for novel view synthesis and scene reconstruction, with applications ranging from large static scenes to dynamic human motion. However, the increased resolution and model-free nature of such neural fields come at the cost of high training times and excessive memory requirements. Recent advances improve the inference time by using complementary data structures yet these methods are ill-suited for dynamic scenes and often increase memory consumption. Little has been done to reduce the resources required at training time. We propose a method to exploit the redundancy of NeRF's sample-based computations by partially sharing evaluations across neighboring sample points. Our UNeRF architecture is inspired by the UNet, where spatial resolution is reduced in the middle of the network and information is shared between adjacent samples. Although this change violates the strict and conscious separation of view-dependent appearance and view-independent density estimation in the NeRF method, we show that it improves novel view synthesis. We also introduce an alternative subsampling strategy which shares computation while minimizing any violation of view invariance. UNeRF is a plug-in module for the original NeRF network. Our major contributions include reduction of the memory footprint, improved accuracy, and reduced amortized processing time both during training and inference. With only weak assumptions on locality, we achieve improved resource utilization on a variety of neural radiance fields tasks. We demonstrate applications to the novel view synthesis of static scenes as well as dynamic human shape and motion.
summary: Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs) increase reconstruction detail for novel view synthesis and scene reconstruction, with applications ranging from large static scenes to dynamic human motion. However, the increased resolution and model-free nature of such neural fields come at the cost of high training times and excessive memory requirements. Recent advances improve the inference time by using complementary data structures yet these methods are ill-suited for dynamic scenes and often increase memory consumption. Little has been done to reduce the resources required at training time. We propose a method to exploit the redundancy of NeRF's sample-based computations by partially sharing evaluations across neighboring sample points. Our UNeRF architecture is inspired by the UNet, where spatial resolution is reduced in the middle of the network and information is shared between adjacent samples. Although this change violates the strict and conscious separation of view-dependent appearance and view-independent density estimation in the NeRF method, we show that it improves novel view synthesis. We also introduce an alternative subsampling strategy which shares computation while minimizing any violation of view invariance. UNeRF is a plug-in module for the original NeRF network. Our major contributions include reduction of the memory footprint, improved accuracy, and reduced amortized processing time both during training and inference. With only weak assumptions on locality, we achieve improved resource utilization on a variety of neural radiance fields tasks. We demonstrate applications to the novel view synthesis of static scenes as well as dynamic human shape and motion.
abstract_zh: 神经辐射场 (NeRF) 增加了新颖视图合成和场景重建的重建细节，其应用范围从大型静态场景到动态人体运动。然而，此类神经场分辨率的提高和无模型特性是以训练时间长和内存需求过多为代价的。最近的进展通过使用互补数据结构改善了推理时间，但这些方法不适合动态场景并且通常会增加内存消耗。几乎没有采取任何措施来减少培训时所需的资源。我们提出了一种方法，通过在相邻样本点之间部分共享评估来利用 NeRF 基于样本的计算的冗余。我们的 UNeRF 架构受到 UNet 的启发，其中空间分辨率在网络中间降低，并且信息在相邻样本之间共享。尽管这种变化违反了 NeRF 方法中视图相关外观和视图无关密度估计的严格且有意识的分离，但我们表明它改进了新颖的视图合成。我们还引入了另一种子采样策略，该策略共享计算，同时最大限度地减少对视图不变性的违反。 UNeRF是原NeRF网络的插件模块。我们的主要贡献包括减少内存占用、提高准确性以及减少训练和推理过程中的摊销处理时间。仅通过对局部性的弱假设，我们就可以提高各种神经辐射场任务的资源利用率。我们演示了静态场景以及动态人体形状和运动的新颖视图合成的应用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出UNeRF，一种通过共享相邻样本计算来减少内存占用和训练时间的U型神经网络架构，用于改进神经辐射场（NeRF）的效率。
AI.motivation: 传统NeRF在高分辨率和无模型特性下存在训练时间长、内存消耗大的问题，现有优化方法难以兼顾动态场景或增加内存负担，亟需降低训练阶段资源消耗。
AI.method: 受UNet启发，UNeRF通过中间层降低空间分辨率并共享相邻样本信息，部分重用计算结果；引入替代子采样策略以最小化视角不变性破坏，作为原NeRF的插件模块。
AI.result: 在静态场景和动态人体重建任务中，UNeRF显著减少内存占用，提升训练与推理速度，同时保持或超越原有重建精度。
AI.conclusion: UNeRF通过局部性假设有效优化资源利用，在保证性能的前提下，为NeRF在动态场景中的应用提供了高效解决方案。

authors_text: Adam Shephard,Mostafa Jahanifar,Ruoyu Wang,Muhammad Dawood,Simon Graham,Kastytis Sidlauskas,Syed Ali Khurram,Nasir Rajpoot,Shan E Ahmed Raza
title: TIAger: Tumor-Infiltrating Lymphocyte Scoring in Breast Cancer for the TiGER Challenge
title_zh: TIAger：针对 TiGER 挑战的乳腺癌肿瘤浸润淋巴细胞评分
abstract: The quantification of tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs) has been shown to be an independent predictor for prognosis of breast cancer patients. Typically, pathologists give an estimate of the proportion of the stromal region that contains TILs to obtain a TILs score. The Tumor InfiltratinG lymphocytes in breast cancER (TiGER) challenge, aims to assess the prognostic significance of computer-generated TILs scores for predicting survival as part of a Cox proportional hazards model. For this challenge, as the TIAger team, we have developed an algorithm to first segment tumor vs. stroma, before localising the tumor bulk region for TILs detection. Finally, we use these outputs to generate a TILs score for each case. On preliminary testing, our approach achieved a tumor-stroma weighted Dice score of 0.791 and a FROC score of 0.572 for lymphocytic detection. For predicting survival, our model achieved a C-index of 0.719. These results achieved first place across the preliminary testing leaderboards of the TiGER challenge.
summary: The quantification of tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs) has been shown to be an independent predictor for prognosis of breast cancer patients. Typically, pathologists give an estimate of the proportion of the stromal region that contains TILs to obtain a TILs score. The Tumor InfiltratinG lymphocytes in breast cancER (TiGER) challenge, aims to assess the prognostic significance of computer-generated TILs scores for predicting survival as part of a Cox proportional hazards model. For this challenge, as the TIAger team, we have developed an algorithm to first segment tumor vs. stroma, before localising the tumor bulk region for TILs detection. Finally, we use these outputs to generate a TILs score for each case. On preliminary testing, our approach achieved a tumor-stroma weighted Dice score of 0.791 and a FROC score of 0.572 for lymphocytic detection. For predicting survival, our model achieved a C-index of 0.719. These results achieved first place across the preliminary testing leaderboards of the TiGER challenge.
abstract_zh: 肿瘤浸润淋巴细胞（TIL）的定量已被证明是乳腺癌患者预后的独立预测因子。 Typically, pathologists give an estimate of the proportion of the stromal region that contains TILs to obtain a TILs score.乳腺癌中的肿瘤浸润淋巴细胞 (TiGER) 挑战旨在评估计算机生成的 TIL 评分对于预测生存的预后意义，作为 Cox 比例风险模型的一部分。针对这一挑战，作为 TIAger 团队，我们开发了一种算法，首先对肿瘤与基质进行分割，然后再定位肿瘤块区域以进行 TIL 检测。 Finally, we use these outputs to generate a TILs score for each case. On preliminary testing, our approach achieved a tumor-stroma weighted Dice score of 0.791 and a FROC score of 0.572 for lymphocytic detection. For predicting survival, our model achieved a C-index of 0.719. These results achieved first place across the preliminary testing leaderboards of the TiGER challenge.

search_text: TIAger: Tumor-Infiltrating Lymphocyte Scoring in Breast Cancer for the TiGER Challenge TIAger：针对 TiGER 挑战的乳腺癌肿瘤浸润淋巴细胞评分 The quantification of tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs) has been shown to be an independent predictor for prognosis of breast cancer patients. Typically, pathologists give an estimate of the proportion of the stromal region that contains TILs to obtain a TILs score. The Tumor InfiltratinG lymphocytes in breast cancER (TiGER) challenge, aims to assess the prognostic significance of computer-generated TILs scores for predicting survival as part of a Cox proportional hazards model. For this challenge, as the TIAger team, we have developed an algorithm to first segment tumor vs. stroma, before localising the tumor bulk region for TILs detection. Finally, we use these outputs to generate a TILs score for each case. On preliminary testing, our approach achieved a tumor-stroma weighted Dice score of 0.791 and a FROC score of 0.572 for lymphocytic detection. For predicting survival, our model achieved a C-index of 0.719. These results achieved first place across the preliminary testing leaderboards of the TiGER challenge. 肿瘤浸润淋巴细胞（TIL）的定量已被证明是乳腺癌患者预后的独立预测因子。 Typically, pathologists give an estimate of the proportion of the stromal region that contains TILs to obtain a TILs score.乳腺癌中的肿瘤浸润淋巴细胞 (TiGER) 挑战旨在评估计算机生成的 TIL 评分对于预测生存的预后意义，作为 Cox 比例风险模型的一部分。针对这一挑战，作为 TIAger 团队，我们开发了一种算法，首先对肿瘤与基质进行分割，然后再定位肿瘤块区域以进行 TIL 检测。 Finally, we use these outputs to generate a TILs score for each case. On preliminary testing, our approach achieved a tumor-stroma weighted Dice score of 0.791 and a FROC score of 0.572 for lymphocytic detection. For predicting survival, our model achieved a C-index of 0.719. These results achieved first place across the preliminary testing leaderboards of the TiGER challenge. 本文提出TIAger算法，用于乳腺癌肿瘤浸润淋巴细胞（TILs）评分，以参与TiGER挑战赛的生存预测评估。 Adam Shephard,Mostafa Jahanifar,Ruoyu Wang,Muhammad Dawood,Simon Graham,Kastytis Sidlauskas,Syed Ali Khurram,Nasir Rajpoot,Shan E Ahmed Raza eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Geraud Nangue Tasse,Benjamin Rosman,Steven James
title: World Value Functions: Knowledge Representation for Learning and Planning
title_zh: 世界价值函数：学习和规划的知识表示
comment: Accepted at the Planning and Reinforcement Learning Workshop at ICAPS 2022. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2205.08827
comments: Accepted at the Planning and Reinforcement Learning Workshop at ICAPS 2022. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2205.08827
abstract: We propose world value functions (WVFs), a type of goal-oriented general value function that represents how to solve not just a given task, but any other goal-reaching task in an agent's environment. This is achieved by equipping an agent with an internal goal space defined as all the world states where it experiences a terminal transition. The agent can then modify the standard task rewards to define its own reward function, which provably drives it to learn how to achieve all reachable internal goals, and the value of doing so in the current task. We demonstrate two key benefits of WVFs in the context of learning and planning. In particular, given a learned WVF, an agent can compute the optimal policy in a new task by simply estimating the task's reward function. Furthermore, we show that WVFs also implicitly encode the transition dynamics of the environment, and so can be used to perform planning. Experimental results show that WVFs can be learned faster than regular value functions, while their ability to infer the environment's dynamics can be used to integrate learning and planning methods to further improve sample efficiency.
summary: We propose world value functions (WVFs), a type of goal-oriented general value function that represents how to solve not just a given task, but any other goal-reaching task in an agent's environment. This is achieved by equipping an agent with an internal goal space defined as all the world states where it experiences a terminal transition. The agent can then modify the standard task rewards to define its own reward function, which provably drives it to learn how to achieve all reachable internal goals, and the value of doing so in the current task. We demonstrate two key benefits of WVFs in the context of learning and planning. In particular, given a learned WVF, an agent can compute the optimal policy in a new task by simply estimating the task's reward function. Furthermore, we show that WVFs also implicitly encode the transition dynamics of the environment, and so can be used to perform planning. Experimental results show that WVFs can be learned faster than regular value functions, while their ability to infer the environment's dynamics can be used to integrate learning and planning methods to further improve sample efficiency.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了世界价值函数（WVF），一种面向目标的通用价值函数，它不仅代表如何解决给定的任务，还代表如何解决代理环境中的任何其他达到目标的任务。 This is achieved by equipping an agent with an internal goal space defined as all the world states where it experiences a terminal transition.然后，代理可以修改标准任务奖励来定义自己的奖励函数，这可以证明驱动它学习如何实现所有可达到的内部目标，以及在当前任务中这样做的价值。 We demonstrate two key benefits of WVFs in the context of learning and planning. In particular, given a learned WVF, an agent can compute the optimal policy in a new task by simply estimating the task's reward function. Furthermore, we show that WVFs also implicitly encode the transition dynamics of the environment, and so can be used to perform planning.实验结果表明，WVF 的学习速度比常规值函数更快，同时其推断环境动态的能力可用于集成学习和规划方法，进一步提高样本效率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出世界价值函数（WVFs），一种用于学习和规划的知识表示方法，使智能体能够解决环境中的任意目标达成任务。
AI.motivation: 传统价值函数仅针对特定任务，难以泛化到其他目标。本文旨在通过统一表示所有可达目标，提升智能体的学习与规划效率。
AI.method: 定义内部目标空间为所有终止状态，通过修改任务奖励函数驱动智能体学习所有可达目标的价值，并利用WVFs隐含的环境动力学进行规划。
AI.result: 实验表明，WVFs比常规价值函数学习更快，且能结合学习与规划方法提升样本效率。
AI.conclusion: WVFs通过统一表示目标与动力学，有效提升了智能体在多样任务中的学习与规划能力。

authors_text: Vedant Nanda,Till Speicher,Camila Kolling,John P. Dickerson,Krishna P. Gummadi,Adrian Weller
title: Measuring Representational Robustness of Neural Networks Through Shared Invariances
title_zh: 通过共享不变性测量神经网络的表征鲁棒性
comment: Accepted for oral presentation at ICML 2022
comments: Accepted for oral presentation at ICML 2022
abstract: A major challenge in studying robustness in deep learning is defining the set of ``meaningless'' perturbations to which a given Neural Network (NN) should be invariant. Most work on robustness implicitly uses a human as the reference model to define such perturbations. Our work offers a new view on robustness by using another reference NN to define the set of perturbations a given NN should be invariant to, thus generalizing the reliance on a reference ``human NN'' to any NN. This makes measuring robustness equivalent to measuring the extent to which two NNs share invariances, for which we propose a measure called STIR. STIR re-purposes existing representation similarity measures to make them suitable for measuring shared invariances. Using our measure, we are able to gain insights into how shared invariances vary with changes in weight initialization, architecture, loss functions, and training dataset. Our implementation is available at: \url{https://github.com/nvedant07/STIR}.
summary: A major challenge in studying robustness in deep learning is defining the set of ``meaningless'' perturbations to which a given Neural Network (NN) should be invariant. Most work on robustness implicitly uses a human as the reference model to define such perturbations. Our work offers a new view on robustness by using another reference NN to define the set of perturbations a given NN should be invariant to, thus generalizing the reliance on a reference ``human NN'' to any NN. This makes measuring robustness equivalent to measuring the extent to which two NNs share invariances, for which we propose a measure called STIR. STIR re-purposes existing representation similarity measures to make them suitable for measuring shared invariances. Using our measure, we are able to gain insights into how shared invariances vary with changes in weight initialization, architecture, loss functions, and training dataset. Our implementation is available at: \url{https://github.com/nvedant07/STIR}.
abstract_zh: A major challenge in studying robustness in deep learning is defining the set of ``meaningless'' perturbations to which a given Neural Network (NN) should be invariant. Most work on robustness implicitly uses a human as the reference model to define such perturbations. Our work offers a new view on robustness by using another reference NN to define the set of perturbations a given NN should be invariant to, thus generalizing the reliance on a reference ``human NN'' to any NN. This makes measuring robustness equivalent to measuring the extent to which two NNs share invariances, for which we propose a measure called STIR. STIR re-purposes existing representation similarity measures to make them suitable for measuring shared invariances.通过我们的测量，我们能够深入了解共享不变性如何随着权重初始化、架构、损失函数和训练数据集的变化而变化。 Our implementation is available at: \url{https://github.com/nvedant07/STIR}.

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过共享不变性衡量神经网络表示鲁棒性的新方法STIR，将传统依赖人类定义扰动的方式扩展到任意神经网络。
AI.motivation: 现有鲁棒性研究依赖人类作为参考模型定义扰动集，但该方法存在局限性。作者希望建立更通用的度量方式，使鲁棒性评估适用于任意神经网络。
AI.method: 使用另一神经网络作为参考模型定义扰动集，基于表示相似性度量设计STIR指标，量化两个网络共享不变性的程度。
AI.result: 实验发现共享不变性随权重初始化、网络架构、损失函数和训练数据集变化而呈现不同特征，验证了STIR的有效性。
AI.conclusion: STIR方法为神经网络鲁棒性研究提供了新视角，能够系统分析不同因素对表示不变性的影响，具有广泛适用性。

method: 使用另一神经网络作为参考模型定义扰动集，基于表示相似性度量设计STIR指标，量化两个网络共享不变性的程度。
result: 实验发现共享不变性随权重初始化、网络架构、损失函数和训练数据集变化而呈现不同特征，验证了STIR的有效性。
conclusion: STIR方法为神经网络鲁棒性研究提供了新视角，能够系统分析不同因素对表示不变性的影响，具有广泛适用性。
search_text: Measuring Representational Robustness of Neural Networks Through Shared Invariances 通过共享不变性测量神经网络的表征鲁棒性 A major challenge in studying robustness in deep learning is defining the set of ``meaningless'' perturbations to which a given Neural Network (NN) should be invariant. Most work on robustness implicitly uses a human as the reference model to define such perturbations. Our work offers a new view on robustness by using another reference NN to define the set of perturbations a given NN should be invariant to, thus generalizing the reliance on a reference ``human NN'' to any NN. This makes measuring robustness equivalent to measuring the extent to which two NNs share invariances, for which we propose a measure called STIR. STIR re-purposes existing representation similarity measures to make them suitable for measuring shared invariances. Using our measure, we are able to gain insights into how shared invariances vary with changes in weight initialization, architecture, loss functions, and training dataset. Our implementation is available at: \url{https://github.com/nvedant07/STIR}. A major challenge in studying robustness in deep learning is defining the set of ``meaningless'' perturbations to which a given Neural Network (NN) should be invariant. Most work on robustness implicitly uses a human as the reference model to define such perturbations. Our work offers a new view on robustness by using another reference NN to define the set of perturbations a given NN should be invariant to, thus generalizing the reliance on a reference ``human NN'' to any NN. This makes measuring robustness equivalent to measuring the extent to which two NNs share invariances, for which we propose a measure called STIR. STIR re-purposes existing representation similarity measures to make them suitable for measuring shared invariances.通过我们的测量，我们能够深入了解共享不变性如何随着权重初始化、架构、损失函数和训练数据集的变化而变化。 Our implementation is available at: \url{https://github.com/nvedant07/STIR}. 本文提出通过共享不变性衡量神经网络表示鲁棒性的新方法STIR，将传统依赖人类定义扰动的方式扩展到任意神经网络。 Vedant Nanda,Till Speicher,Camila Kolling,John P. Dickerson,Krishna P. Gummadi,Adrian Weller cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Mike Walmsley,Inigo Val Slijepcevic,Micah Bowles,Anna M. M. Scaife
title: Towards Galaxy Foundation Models with Hybrid Contrastive Learning
title_zh: 通过混合对比学习构建 Galaxy Foundation 模型
comment: Accepted at the ICML 2022 Workshop on Machine Learning for Astrophysics. Data: www.github.com/mwalmsley/pytorch-galaxy-datasets. Please reach out to share your labelled data - all contributions will be credited in future work
comments: Accepted at the ICML 2022 Workshop on Machine Learning for Astrophysics. Data: www.github.com/mwalmsley/pytorch-galaxy-datasets. Please reach out to share your labelled data - all contributions will be credited in future work
abstract: New astronomical tasks are often related to earlier tasks for which labels have already been collected. We adapt the contrastive framework BYOL to leverage those labels as a pretraining task while also enforcing augmentation invariance. For large-scale pretraining, we introduce GZ-Evo v0.1, a set of 96.5M volunteer responses for 552k galaxy images plus a further 1.34M comparable unlabelled galaxies. Most of the 206 GZ-Evo answers are unknown for any given galaxy, and so our pretraining task uses a Dirichlet loss that naturally handles unknown answers. GZ-Evo pretraining, with or without hybrid learning, improves on direct training even with plentiful downstream labels (+4% accuracy with 44k labels). Our hybrid pretraining/contrastive method further improves downstream accuracy vs. pretraining or contrastive learning, especially in the low-label transfer regime (+6% accuracy with 750 labels).
summary: New astronomical tasks are often related to earlier tasks for which labels have already been collected. We adapt the contrastive framework BYOL to leverage those labels as a pretraining task while also enforcing augmentation invariance. For large-scale pretraining, we introduce GZ-Evo v0.1, a set of 96.5M volunteer responses for 552k galaxy images plus a further 1.34M comparable unlabelled galaxies. Most of the 206 GZ-Evo answers are unknown for any given galaxy, and so our pretraining task uses a Dirichlet loss that naturally handles unknown answers. GZ-Evo pretraining, with or without hybrid learning, improves on direct training even with plentiful downstream labels (+4% accuracy with 44k labels). Our hybrid pretraining/contrastive method further improves downstream accuracy vs. pretraining or contrastive learning, especially in the low-label transfer regime (+6% accuracy with 750 labels).
abstract_zh: 新的天文任务通常与已经收集标签的早期任务相关。我们调整对比框架 BYOL 以利用这些标签作为预训练任务，同时还强制增强不变性。对于大规模预训练，我们引入了 GZ-Evo v0.1，这是一组针对 552k 星系图像的 9650 万志愿者响应，以及另外 134 万个可比较的未标记星系。对于任何给定的星系，206 个 GZ-Evo 答案中的大多数都是未知的，因此我们的预训练任务使用自然处理未知答案的狄利克雷损失。无论有或没有混合学习，GZ-Evo 预训练都可以改进直接训练，即使有大量下游标签（44k 标签的准确率+4%）。与预训练或对比学习相比，我们的混合预训练/对比方法进一步提高了下游准确性，特别是在低标签传输机制中（750 个标签的准确性提高 6%）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种混合对比学习方法，利用现有标签和未标记星系数据预训练基础模型，以提升下游天文学任务的准确性。
AI.motivation: 新天文学任务常依赖已有标签数据，但直接训练效果受限。需利用未标记数据与标签数据结合，解决标签稀缺问题。
AI.method: 基于BYOL框架，引入GZ-Evo v0.1数据集（含96.5M志愿者回答及1.34M未标记星系），采用Dirichlet损失处理未知答案，并结合预训练与对比学习。
AI.result: 预训练模型在下游任务中提升4%准确率（44k标签）及6%（750标签），混合方法优于单独预训练或对比学习。
AI.conclusion: 混合对比学习能有效提升星系模型性能，尤其在低标签场景下，预训练与对比学习结合具有显著优势。

authors_text: Nicholas Kluge Corrêa,Camila Galvão,James William Santos,Carolina Del Pino,Edson Pontes Pinto,Camila Barbosa,Diogo Massmann,Rodrigo Mambrini,Luiza Galvão,Edmund Terem,Nythamar de Oliveira
title: Worldwide AI Ethics: a review of 200 guidelines and recommendations for AI governance
title_zh: 全球人工智能道德：对 200 条人工智能治理指南和建议的回顾
abstract: The utilization of artificial intelligence (AI) applications has experienced tremendous growth in recent years, bringing forth numerous benefits and conveniences. However, this expansion has also provoked ethical concerns, such as privacy breaches, algorithmic discrimination, security and reliability issues, transparency, and other unintended consequences. To determine whether a global consensus exists regarding the ethical principles that should govern AI applications and to contribute to the formation of future regulations, this paper conducts a meta-analysis of 200 governance policies and ethical guidelines for AI usage published by public bodies, academic institutions, private companies, and civil society organizations worldwide. We identified at least 17 resonating principles prevalent in the policies and guidelines of our dataset, released as an open-source database and tool. We present the limitations of performing a global scale analysis study paired with a critical analysis of our findings, presenting areas of consensus that should be incorporated into future regulatory efforts. All components tied to this work can be found in https://nkluge-correa.github.io/worldwide_AI-ethics/
summary: The utilization of artificial intelligence (AI) applications has experienced tremendous growth in recent years, bringing forth numerous benefits and conveniences. However, this expansion has also provoked ethical concerns, such as privacy breaches, algorithmic discrimination, security and reliability issues, transparency, and other unintended consequences. To determine whether a global consensus exists regarding the ethical principles that should govern AI applications and to contribute to the formation of future regulations, this paper conducts a meta-analysis of 200 governance policies and ethical guidelines for AI usage published by public bodies, academic institutions, private companies, and civil society organizations worldwide. We identified at least 17 resonating principles prevalent in the policies and guidelines of our dataset, released as an open-source database and tool. We present the limitations of performing a global scale analysis study paired with a critical analysis of our findings, presenting areas of consensus that should be incorporated into future regulatory efforts. All components tied to this work can be found in https://nkluge-correa.github.io/worldwide_AI-ethics/
abstract_zh: 近年来，人工智能（AI）应用的使用经历了巨大的增长，带来了众多的好处和便利。然而，这种扩张也引发了道德问题，例如隐私泄露、算法歧视、安全性和可靠性问题、透明度和其他意想不到的后果。为了确定是否存在全球共识来指导人工智能应用的道德原则，并为未来法规的制定做出贡献，本文对全球公共机构、学术机构、私营公司和民间社会组织发布的 200 项人工智能使用治理政策和道德准则进行了荟萃分析。我们确定了数据集政策和指南中普遍存在的至少 17 条引起共鸣的原则，并以开源数据库和工具的形式发布。我们提出了进行全球规模分析研究的局限性，并对我们的研究结果进行了批判性分析，提出了应纳入未来监管工作的共识领域。与这项工作相关的所有组件都可以在 https://nkluge-correa.github.io/worldwide_AI-ethics/ 中找到

AI.tldr: 审查全球200份AI伦理指南，确定共同原则以指导未来治理
AI.motivation: AI应用的快速增长引发隐私、算法歧视等伦理问题，需确定全球共识以指导未来法规
AI.method: 对全球200份AI治理政策和伦理指南进行元分析，识别共同原则并建立开放数据库
AI.result: 识别出17个普遍原则，建立开放数据库，指出共识领域及分析局限性
AI.conclusion: 提出未来法规应纳入这些原则，同时指出全球分析的局限性

authors_text: Zhicheng Yang,Jui-Hsin Lai,Jun Zhou,Hang Zhou,Chen Du,Zhongcheng Lai
title: Agriculture-Vision Challenge 2022 -- The Runner-Up Solution for Agricultural Pattern Recognition via Transformer-based Models
title_zh: 2022 年农业视觉挑战赛——基于 Transformer 模型的农业模式识别亚军解决方案
abstract: The Agriculture-Vision Challenge in CVPR is one of the most famous and competitive challenges for global researchers to break the boundary between computer vision and agriculture sectors, aiming at agricultural pattern recognition from aerial images. In this paper, we propose our solution to the third Agriculture-Vision Challenge in CVPR 2022. We leverage a data pre-processing scheme and several Transformer-based models as well as data augmentation techniques to achieve a mIoU of 0.582, accomplishing the 2nd place in this challenge.
summary: The Agriculture-Vision Challenge in CVPR is one of the most famous and competitive challenges for global researchers to break the boundary between computer vision and agriculture sectors, aiming at agricultural pattern recognition from aerial images. In this paper, we propose our solution to the third Agriculture-Vision Challenge in CVPR 2022. We leverage a data pre-processing scheme and several Transformer-based models as well as data augmentation techniques to achieve a mIoU of 0.582, accomplishing the 2nd place in this challenge.
abstract_zh: CVPR 中的农业视觉挑战赛是全球研究人员最著名、最具竞争力的挑战赛之一，旨在打破计算机视觉和农业领域之间的界限，旨在从航空图像中识别农业模式。在本文中，我们提出了 CVPR 2022 第三届农业视觉挑战赛的解决方案。我们利用数据预处理方案和多个基于 Transformer 的模型以及数据增强技术，实现了 0.582 的 mIoU，在该挑战赛中获得第二名。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于Transformer模型的农业模式识别解决方案，用于CVPR 2022农业视觉挑战赛。
AI.motivation: 推动计算机视觉与农业领域的结合，解决航拍图像中的农业模式识别难题。
AI.method: 采用数据预处理方案、Transformer模型及数据增强技术相结合的方法。
AI.result: 在挑战赛中以mIoU 0.582的成绩获得亚军。
AI.conclusion: Transformer模型在农业视觉任务中展现潜力，数据预处理与增强技术对提升性能至关重要。

search_text: Agriculture-Vision Challenge 2022 -- The Runner-Up Solution for Agricultural Pattern Recognition via Transformer-based Models 2022 年农业视觉挑战赛——基于 Transformer 模型的农业模式识别亚军解决方案 The Agriculture-Vision Challenge in CVPR is one of the most famous and competitive challenges for global researchers to break the boundary between computer vision and agriculture sectors, aiming at agricultural pattern recognition from aerial images. In this paper, we propose our solution to the third Agriculture-Vision Challenge in CVPR 2022. We leverage a data pre-processing scheme and several Transformer-based models as well as data augmentation techniques to achieve a mIoU of 0.582, accomplishing the 2nd place in this challenge. CVPR 中的农业视觉挑战赛是全球研究人员最著名、最具竞争力的挑战赛之一，旨在打破计算机视觉和农业领域之间的界限，旨在从航空图像中识别农业模式。在本文中，我们提出了 CVPR 2022 第三届农业视觉挑战赛的解决方案。我们利用数据预处理方案和多个基于 Transformer 的模型以及数据增强技术，实现了 0.582 的 mIoU，在该挑战赛中获得第二名。 本文提出了一种基于Transformer模型的农业模式识别解决方案，用于CVPR 2022农业视觉挑战赛。 Zhicheng Yang,Jui-Hsin Lai,Jun Zhou,Hang Zhou,Chen Du,Zhongcheng Lai cs.CV

authors_text: Ryma Boumazouza,Fahima Cheikh-Alili,Bertrand Mazure,Karim Tabia
title: ASTERYX : A model-Agnostic SaT-basEd appRoach for sYmbolic and score-based eXplanations
title_zh: ASTERYX：一种与模型无关的基于卫星的符号和基于分数的解释方法
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2206.11539
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2206.11539
abstract: The ever increasing complexity of machine learning techniques used more and more in practice, gives rise to the need to explain the predictions and decisions of these models, often used as black-boxes. Explainable AI approaches are either numerical feature-based aiming to quantify the contribution of each feature in a prediction or symbolic providing certain forms of symbolic explanations such as counterfactuals. This paper proposes a generic agnostic approach named ASTERYX allowing to generate both symbolic explanations and score-based ones. Our approach is declarative and it is based on the encoding of the model to be explained in an equivalent symbolic representation, this latter serves to generate in particular two types of symbolic explanations which are sufficient reasons and counterfactuals. We then associate scores reflecting the relevance of the explanations and the features w.r.t to some properties. Our experimental results show the feasibility of the proposed approach and its effectiveness in providing symbolic and score-based explanations.
summary: The ever increasing complexity of machine learning techniques used more and more in practice, gives rise to the need to explain the predictions and decisions of these models, often used as black-boxes. Explainable AI approaches are either numerical feature-based aiming to quantify the contribution of each feature in a prediction or symbolic providing certain forms of symbolic explanations such as counterfactuals. This paper proposes a generic agnostic approach named ASTERYX allowing to generate both symbolic explanations and score-based ones. Our approach is declarative and it is based on the encoding of the model to be explained in an equivalent symbolic representation, this latter serves to generate in particular two types of symbolic explanations which are sufficient reasons and counterfactuals. We then associate scores reflecting the relevance of the explanations and the features w.r.t to some properties. Our experimental results show the feasibility of the proposed approach and its effectiveness in providing symbolic and score-based explanations.
abstract_zh: 在实践中越来越多地使用的机器学习技术的复杂性不断增加，因此需要解释这些通常用作黑匣子的模型的预测和决策。可解释的人工智能方法要么是基于数字特征的，旨在量化每个特征在预测中的贡献，要么是提供某些形式的符号解释（例如反事实）的符号方法。本文提出了一种名为 ASTERYX 的通用不可知论方法，允许生成符号解释和基于分数的解释。我们的方法是声明性的，它基于要以等效符号表示来解释的模型的编码，后者特别用于生成两种类型的符号解释，即充分理由和反事实。然后，我们将反映解释和特征与某些属性的相关性的分数关联起来。我们的实验结果表明了所提出方法的可行性及其在提供符号和基于分数的解释方面的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为ASTERIX的模型无关方法，用于生成符号化和基于分数的机器学习模型解释。
AI.motivation: 随着机器学习模型复杂度增加，其作为黑箱的特性导致需要解释预测决策。现有方法仅能提供数值特征或符号化解释，缺乏统一解决方案。
AI.method: 通过将模型编码为等效符号表示，结合SAT求解技术生成充分理由和反事实等符号解释，并关联分数反映解释与特征的相关性。
AI.result: 实验验证了该方法在生成符号化和基于分数解释方面的可行性与有效性。
AI.conclusion: ASTERIX为可解释AI提供了统一框架，能够同时生成两种类型的解释，满足复杂模型的解释需求。

authors_text: Stuart Reeves
title: Navigating Incommensurability Between Ethnomethodology, Conversation Analysis, and Artificial Intelligence
title_zh: 探索民族方法学、对话分析和人工智能之间的不可通约性
abstract: Like many research communities, ethnomethodologists and conversation analysts have begun to get caught up -- yet again -- in the pervasive spectacle of surging interests in Artificial Intelligence (AI). Inspired by discussions amongst a growing network of researchers in ethnomethodology (EM) and conversation analysis (CA) traditions who nurse such interests, I started thinking about what things EM and the more EM end of conversation analysis might be doing about, for, or even with, fields of AI research. So, this piece is about the disciplinary and conceptual questions that might be encountered, and -- in my view -- may need addressing for engagements with AI research and its affiliates. Although I'm mostly concerned with things to be aware of as well as outright dangers, later on we can think about some opportunities. And throughout I will keep using 'we' to talk about EM&CA researchers; but this really is for convenience only -- I don't wish to ventriloquise for our complex research communities. All of the following should be read as emanating from my particular research history, standpoint etc., and treated (hopefully) as an invitation for further discussion amongst EM and CA researchers turning to technology and AI specifically.
summary: Like many research communities, ethnomethodologists and conversation analysts have begun to get caught up -- yet again -- in the pervasive spectacle of surging interests in Artificial Intelligence (AI). Inspired by discussions amongst a growing network of researchers in ethnomethodology (EM) and conversation analysis (CA) traditions who nurse such interests, I started thinking about what things EM and the more EM end of conversation analysis might be doing about, for, or even with, fields of AI research. So, this piece is about the disciplinary and conceptual questions that might be encountered, and -- in my view -- may need addressing for engagements with AI research and its affiliates. Although I'm mostly concerned with things to be aware of as well as outright dangers, later on we can think about some opportunities. And throughout I will keep using 'we' to talk about EM&CA researchers; but this really is for convenience only -- I don't wish to ventriloquise for our complex research communities. All of the following should be read as emanating from my particular research history, standpoint etc., and treated (hopefully) as an invitation for further discussion amongst EM and CA researchers turning to technology and AI specifically.
abstract_zh: 与许多研究团体一样，民族方法学家和对话分析师再次开始陷入对人工智能 (AI) 兴趣高涨的普遍景象。受到越来越多的民族方法论 (EM) 和对话分析 (CA) 传统研究人员网络中关注此类兴趣的研究人员网络中的讨论的启发，我开始思考 EM 和对话分析的更多 EM 端可能会为人工智能研究领域做些什么，甚至与人工智能研究领域一起做些什么。因此，这篇文章是关于可能遇到的学科和概念问题，并且在我看来，在与人工智能研究及其附属机构的合作中可能需要解决这些问题。虽然我最关心的是需要注意的事情以及直接的危险，但稍后我们可以考虑一些机会。在整个过程中，我将继续使用“我们”来谈论 EM&CA 研究人员；但这确实只是为了方便——我不想为我们复杂的研究团体进行口技表演。以下所有内容都应被理解为源自我的特定研究历史、观点等，并（希望）被视为邀请专门针对技术和人工智能的 EM 和 CA 研究人员进行进一步讨论。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了民族方法学（EM）与会话分析（CA）在与人工智能（AI）研究互动时面临的学科与概念性挑战，并提出需要关注的问题与潜在机遇。
AI.motivation: 随着AI研究的兴起，EM与CA研究者需重新审视其学科框架与AI的关联，明确互动中的潜在风险与研究方向。
AI.method: 通过反思性讨论与文献分析，结合研究者个人视角，梳理EM/CA与AI交叉领域的关键议题。
AI.result: 识别出EM/CA与AI互动中的概念分歧、方法论冲突及伦理风险，同时提出跨学科对话的可能路径。
AI.conclusion: EM/CA研究者需保持批判性思维，在与AI领域互动时注重学科独特性，推动更深入的跨学科合作与理论创新。

authors_text: Viktor Rudnev,Mohamed Elgharib,Christian Theobalt,Vladislav Golyanik
title: EventNeRF: Neural Radiance Fields from a Single Colour Event Camera
title_zh: EventNeRF：来自单色事件相机的神经辐射场
abstract: Asynchronously operating event cameras find many applications due to their high dynamic range, vanishingly low motion blur, low latency and low data bandwidth. The field saw remarkable progress during the last few years, and existing event-based 3D reconstruction approaches recover sparse point clouds of the scene. However, such sparsity is a limiting factor in many cases, especially in computer vision and graphics, that has not been addressed satisfactorily so far. Accordingly, this paper proposes the first approach for 3D-consistent, dense and photorealistic novel view synthesis using just a single colour event stream as input. At its core is a neural radiance field trained entirely in a self-supervised manner from events while preserving the original resolution of the colour event channels. Next, our ray sampling strategy is tailored to events and allows for data-efficient training. At test, our method produces results in the RGB space at unprecedented quality. We evaluate our method qualitatively and numerically on several challenging synthetic and real scenes and show that it produces significantly denser and more visually appealing renderings than the existing methods. We also demonstrate robustness in challenging scenarios with fast motion and under low lighting conditions. We release the newly recorded dataset and our source code to facilitate the research field, see https://4dqv.mpi-inf.mpg.de/EventNeRF.
summary: Asynchronously operating event cameras find many applications due to their high dynamic range, vanishingly low motion blur, low latency and low data bandwidth. The field saw remarkable progress during the last few years, and existing event-based 3D reconstruction approaches recover sparse point clouds of the scene. However, such sparsity is a limiting factor in many cases, especially in computer vision and graphics, that has not been addressed satisfactorily so far. Accordingly, this paper proposes the first approach for 3D-consistent, dense and photorealistic novel view synthesis using just a single colour event stream as input. At its core is a neural radiance field trained entirely in a self-supervised manner from events while preserving the original resolution of the colour event channels. Next, our ray sampling strategy is tailored to events and allows for data-efficient training. At test, our method produces results in the RGB space at unprecedented quality. We evaluate our method qualitatively and numerically on several challenging synthetic and real scenes and show that it produces significantly denser and more visually appealing renderings than the existing methods. We also demonstrate robustness in challenging scenarios with fast motion and under low lighting conditions. We release the newly recorded dataset and our source code to facilitate the research field, see https://4dqv.mpi-inf.mpg.de/EventNeRF.
abstract_zh: 异步操作事件摄像机因其高动态范围、极低的运动模糊、低延迟和低数据带宽而获得许多应用。该领域在过去几年中取得了显着进展，现有的基于事件的 3D 重建方法可以恢复场景的稀疏点云。然而，这种稀疏性在许多情况下是一个限制因素，特别是在计算机视觉和图形领域，迄今为止尚未得到令人满意的解决。因此，本文提出了第一种仅使用单一颜色事件流作为输入来进行 3D 一致、密集且逼真的新颖视图合成的方法。其核心是完全以自监督方式从事件中训练的神经辐射场，同时保留颜色事件通道的原始分辨率。接下来，我们的射线采样策略根据事件量身定制，并允许进行数据高效的训练。在测试中，我们的方法在 RGB 空间中以前所未有的质量生成结果。我们在几个具有挑战性的合成和真实场景上对我们的方法进行定性和数值评估，并表明它比现有方法产生明显更密集和更具视觉吸引力的渲染。我们还在快速运动和低光照条件下的挑战性场景中展示了鲁棒性。我们发布了新记录的数据集和源代码以促进研究领域的发展，请参阅https://4dqv.mpi-inf.mpg.de/EventNeRF。

AI.tldr: 本文提出EventNeRF方法，利用单色事件相机流实现高保真、稠密的三维场景重建与新视角合成。
AI.motivation: 现有事件相机三维重建方法生成的点云稀疏，难以满足计算机视觉与图形学需求，亟需更稠密且视觉逼真的重建方案。
AI.method: 基于自监督训练的神经辐射场（NeRF），结合事件相机特性设计数据高效采样策略，保留原始色彩分辨率并实现端到端重建。
AI.result: 在合成与真实场景中生成比现有方法更稠密、视觉效果更优的渲染结果，且在高速运动与低光照场景下表现稳健，并发布新数据集与开源代码。
AI.conclusion: EventNeRF首次实现单事件流的稠密高质量三维重建，为事件相机在视觉与图形领域的应用提供新范式与基础资源。

search_text: EventNeRF: Neural Radiance Fields from a Single Colour Event Camera EventNeRF：来自单色事件相机的神经辐射场 Asynchronously operating event cameras find many applications due to their high dynamic range, vanishingly low motion blur, low latency and low data bandwidth. The field saw remarkable progress during the last few years, and existing event-based 3D reconstruction approaches recover sparse point clouds of the scene. However, such sparsity is a limiting factor in many cases, especially in computer vision and graphics, that has not been addressed satisfactorily so far. Accordingly, this paper proposes the first approach for 3D-consistent, dense and photorealistic novel view synthesis using just a single colour event stream as input. At its core is a neural radiance field trained entirely in a self-supervised manner from events while preserving the original resolution of the colour event channels. Next, our ray sampling strategy is tailored to events and allows for data-efficient training. At test, our method produces results in the RGB space at unprecedented quality. We evaluate our method qualitatively and numerically on several challenging synthetic and real scenes and show that it produces significantly denser and more visually appealing renderings than the existing methods. We also demonstrate robustness in challenging scenarios with fast motion and under low lighting conditions. We release the newly recorded dataset and our source code to facilitate the research field, see https://4dqv.mpi-inf.mpg.de/EventNeRF. 异步操作事件摄像机因其高动态范围、极低的运动模糊、低延迟和低数据带宽而获得许多应用。该领域在过去几年中取得了显着进展，现有的基于事件的 3D 重建方法可以恢复场景的稀疏点云。然而，这种稀疏性在许多情况下是一个限制因素，特别是在计算机视觉和图形领域，迄今为止尚未得到令人满意的解决。因此，本文提出了第一种仅使用单一颜色事件流作为输入来进行 3D 一致、密集且逼真的新颖视图合成的方法。其核心是完全以自监督方式从事件中训练的神经辐射场，同时保留颜色事件通道的原始分辨率。接下来，我们的射线采样策略根据事件量身定制，并允许进行数据高效的训练。在测试中，我们的方法在 RGB 空间中以前所未有的质量生成结果。我们在几个具有挑战性的合成和真实场景上对我们的方法进行定性和数值评估，并表明它比现有方法产生明显更密集和更具视觉吸引力的渲染。我们还在快速运动和低光照条件下的挑战性场景中展示了鲁棒性。我们发布了新记录的数据集和源代码以促进研究领域的发展，请参阅https://4dqv.mpi-inf.mpg.de/EventNeRF。 本文提出EventNeRF方法，利用单色事件相机流实现高保真、稠密的三维场景重建与新视角合成。 Viktor Rudnev,Mohamed Elgharib,Christian Theobalt,Vladislav Golyanik cs.CV

authors_text: Jinghuan Shang,Srijan Das,Michael S. Ryoo
title: Learning Viewpoint-Agnostic Visual Representations by Recovering Tokens in 3D Space
title_zh: 通过恢复 3D 空间中的标记来学习与视点无关的视觉表示
comment: NeurIPS 2022. Our code is at https://github.com/elicassion/3DTRL Our project page is at https://www3.cs.stonybrook.edu/~jishang/3dtrl/3dtrl.html v3, v4 for minor updates on figures and visualizations
comments: NeurIPS 2022. Our code is at https://github.com/elicassion/3DTRL Our project page is at https://www3.cs.stonybrook.edu/~jishang/3dtrl/3dtrl.html v3, v4 for minor updates on figures and visualizations
abstract: Humans are remarkably flexible in understanding viewpoint changes due to visual cortex supporting the perception of 3D structure. In contrast, most of the computer vision models that learn visual representation from a pool of 2D images often fail to generalize over novel camera viewpoints. Recently, the vision architectures have shifted towards convolution-free architectures, visual Transformers, which operate on tokens derived from image patches. However, these Transformers do not perform explicit operations to learn viewpoint-agnostic representation for visual understanding. To this end, we propose a 3D Token Representation Layer (3DTRL) that estimates the 3D positional information of the visual tokens and leverages it for learning viewpoint-agnostic representations. The key elements of 3DTRL include a pseudo-depth estimator and a learned camera matrix to impose geometric transformations on the tokens, trained in an unsupervised fashion. These enable 3DTRL to recover the 3D positional information of the tokens from 2D patches. In practice, 3DTRL is easily plugged-in into a Transformer. Our experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of 3DTRL in many vision tasks including image classification, multi-view video alignment, and action recognition. The models with 3DTRL outperform their backbone Transformers in all the tasks with minimal added computation. Our code is available at https://github.com/elicassion/3DTRL.
summary: Humans are remarkably flexible in understanding viewpoint changes due to visual cortex supporting the perception of 3D structure. In contrast, most of the computer vision models that learn visual representation from a pool of 2D images often fail to generalize over novel camera viewpoints. Recently, the vision architectures have shifted towards convolution-free architectures, visual Transformers, which operate on tokens derived from image patches. However, these Transformers do not perform explicit operations to learn viewpoint-agnostic representation for visual understanding. To this end, we propose a 3D Token Representation Layer (3DTRL) that estimates the 3D positional information of the visual tokens and leverages it for learning viewpoint-agnostic representations. The key elements of 3DTRL include a pseudo-depth estimator and a learned camera matrix to impose geometric transformations on the tokens, trained in an unsupervised fashion. These enable 3DTRL to recover the 3D positional information of the tokens from 2D patches. In practice, 3DTRL is easily plugged-in into a Transformer. Our experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of 3DTRL in many vision tasks including image classification, multi-view video alignment, and action recognition. The models with 3DTRL outperform their backbone Transformers in all the tasks with minimal added computation. Our code is available at https://github.com/elicassion/3DTRL.
abstract_zh: 由于视觉皮层支持 3D 结构的感知，人类在理解视点变化方面非常灵活。相比之下，大多数从 2D 图像池中学习视觉表示的计算机视觉模型通常无法概括新颖的相机视点。最近，视觉架构已经转向无卷积架构，即视觉 Transformer，它对从图像补丁派生的标记进行操作。然而，这些 Transformer 不会执行显式操作来学习与视点无关的表示以进行视觉理解。为此，我们提出了一个 3D 令牌表示层（3DTRL），它估计视觉令牌的 3D 位置信息，并利用它来学习与视点无关的表示。 3DTRL 的关键要素包括伪深度估计器和学习相机矩阵，用于对标记进行几何变换，并以无监督方式进行训练。这些使 3DTRL 能够从 2D 补丁中恢复标记的 3D 位置信息。在实践中，3DTRL 可以轻松插入到 Transformer 中。我们的实验证明了 3DTRL 在许多视觉任务中的有效性，包括图像分类、多视图视频对齐和动作识别。具有 3DTRL 的模型在所有任务中都优于其骨干 Transformer，并且增加的计算量最少。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/elicassion/3DTRL 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为3D Token Representation Layer (3DTRL) 的方法，通过从2D图像中恢复视觉标记的3D位置信息，学习视角无关的视觉表示。
AI.motivation: 人类能灵活理解视角变化，但现有基于2D图像的视觉模型在新视角下表现不佳。视觉Transformer虽广泛应用，但未显式学习视角无关的表示，限制了其泛化能力。
AI.method: 设计3DTRL层，包含伪深度估计器和学习相机矩阵，通过无监督方式对视觉标记施加几何变换，从2D图像块中恢复3D位置信息，并可无缝集成到Transformer架构中。
AI.result: 实验表明，集成3DTRL的模型在图像分类、多视角视频对齐和动作识别等任务中均优于原始Transformer，且计算开销极小。
AI.conclusion: 3DTRL通过显式建模3D几何信息，有效提升了视觉模型的视角不变性，为多视角视觉任务提供了高效解决方案。

search_text: Learning Viewpoint-Agnostic Visual Representations by Recovering Tokens in 3D Space 通过恢复 3D 空间中的标记来学习与视点无关的视觉表示 Humans are remarkably flexible in understanding viewpoint changes due to visual cortex supporting the perception of 3D structure. In contrast, most of the computer vision models that learn visual representation from a pool of 2D images often fail to generalize over novel camera viewpoints. Recently, the vision architectures have shifted towards convolution-free architectures, visual Transformers, which operate on tokens derived from image patches. However, these Transformers do not perform explicit operations to learn viewpoint-agnostic representation for visual understanding. To this end, we propose a 3D Token Representation Layer (3DTRL) that estimates the 3D positional information of the visual tokens and leverages it for learning viewpoint-agnostic representations. The key elements of 3DTRL include a pseudo-depth estimator and a learned camera matrix to impose geometric transformations on the tokens, trained in an unsupervised fashion. These enable 3DTRL to recover the 3D positional information of the tokens from 2D patches. In practice, 3DTRL is easily plugged-in into a Transformer. Our experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of 3DTRL in many vision tasks including image classification, multi-view video alignment, and action recognition. The models with 3DTRL outperform their backbone Transformers in all the tasks with minimal added computation. authors_text: Agrim Gupta,Stephen Tian,Yunzhi Zhang,Jiajun Wu,Roberto Martín-Martín,Li Fei-Fei
title: MaskViT: Masked Visual Pre-Training for Video Prediction
title_zh: MaskViT：用于视频预测的蒙版视觉预训练
abstract: The ability to predict future visual observations conditioned on past observations and motor commands can enable embodied agents to plan solutions to a variety of tasks in complex environments. This work shows that we can create good video prediction models by pre-training transformers via masked visual modeling. Our approach, named MaskViT, is based on two simple design decisions. First, for memory and training efficiency, we use two types of window attention: spatial and spatiotemporal. Second, during training, we mask a variable percentage of tokens instead of a fixed mask ratio. For inference, MaskViT generates all tokens via iterative refinement where we incrementally decrease the masking ratio following a mask scheduling function. On several datasets we demonstrate that MaskViT outperforms prior works in video prediction, is parameter efficient, and can generate high-resolution videos (256x256). Further, we demonstrate the benefits of inference speedup (up to 512x) due to iterative decoding by using MaskViT for planning on a real robot. Our work suggests that we can endow embodied agents with powerful predictive models by leveraging the general framework of masked visual modeling with minimal domain knowledge.
summary: The ability to predict future visual observations conditioned on past observations and motor commands can enable embodied agents to plan solutions to a variety of tasks in complex environments. This work shows that we can create good video prediction models by pre-training transformers via masked visual modeling. Our approach, named MaskViT, is based on two simple design decisions. First, for memory and training efficiency, we use two types of window attention: spatial and spatiotemporal. Second, during training, we mask a variable percentage of tokens instead of a fixed mask ratio. For inference, MaskViT generates all tokens via iterative refinement where we incrementally decrease the masking ratio following a mask scheduling function. On several datasets we demonstrate that MaskViT outperforms prior works in video prediction, is parameter efficient, and can generate high-resolution videos (256x256). Further, we demonstrate the benefits of inference speedup (up to 512x) due to iterative decoding by using MaskViT for planning on a real robot. Our work suggests that we can endow embodied agents with powerful predictive models by leveraging the general framework of masked visual modeling with minimal domain knowledge.
abstract_zh: 根据过去的观察和运动命令来预测未来视觉观察的能力可以使实体代理能够为复杂环境中的各种任务规划解决方案。这项工作表明，我们可以通过蒙版视觉建模预训练变压器来创建良好的视频预测模型。我们的方法名为 MaskViT，基于两个简单的设计决策。首先，为了记忆和训练效率，我们使用两种类型的窗口注意力：空间注意力和时空注意力。其次，在训练期间，我们屏蔽可变百分比的标记，而不是固定的屏蔽比率。为了进行推理，MaskViT 通过迭代细化生成所有标记，其中我们按照掩码调度函数逐步降低掩码率。在多个数据集上，我们证明 MaskViT 在视频预测方面优于先前的工作，参数效率高，并且可以生成高分辨率视频 (256x256)。此外，我们还展示了通过使用 MaskViT 规划真实机器人进行迭代解码而带来的推理加速（高达 512 倍）的优势。我们的工作表明，我们可以通过利用具有最少领域知识的屏蔽视觉建模的通用框架，赋予实体代理强大的预测模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出MaskViT，一种基于遮罩视觉预训练的视频预测模型，用于具身智能体的未来视觉预测。
AI.motivation: 预测未来视觉观察对具身智能体在复杂环境中执行任务规划至关重要，但现有方法存在效率或效果不足的问题。
AI.method: 采用空间和时空窗口注意力提升效率，训练时使用可变遮罩比例，推理阶段通过迭代优化逐步降低遮罩比例生成视频。
AI.result: 在多个数据集上优于现有方法，参数高效，支持256x256高分辨率视频生成，并实现最高512倍推理加速的机器人规划验证。
AI.conclusion: 遮罩视觉建模框架可赋予具身智能体强大预测能力，且仅需最少领域知识。
